title: CYLINDER BLOCK — Inspection (Cont'd) — BEARING CLEARANCE

figures_detected.description: Exploded/layout diagram of main bearings showing upper bearing, thrust bearing (only upper), No.5 bearing, oil hole, lower bearing, identification color, oil groove, front direction, and No.1 bearing positions
Photo showing main bearing cap installed on cylinder block with measuring equipment for inner diameter measurement
Photo showing measurement of outer diameter of crankshaft main journal using a micrometer
Diagram showing crank pin/journal fillet dimensions: CG10DE R2.3-2.7 mm, CG13DE R1.5 mm, with fillet radius R2.3-2.7 mm indicated

steps.text: Set main bearings in their proper positions on cylinder block and main bearing cap.
Install main bearing cap to cylinder block. Tighten all bolts in correct order in two or three stages.
notes: Tighten all bolts in correct order in two or three stages.

steps.text: Measure inner diameter of each main bearing.
Measure outer diameter of each main journal in crankshaft.
Calculate main bearing clearance. Main bearing clearance = Inner dia. - Outer dia. Standard: 0.010 - 0.044 mm (0.0004 - 0.0017 in). Limit: 0.064 mm (0.0025 in).
If it exceeds the limit, replace bearing.
If clearance cannot be adjusted within the standard of any bearing, grind crankshaft journal and use undersized bearing.

content_blocks.text: When grinding crank pin and crank journal:
a. Grind until clearance is within specified standard bearing clearance.
b. Fillets should be finished as shown in the figure. Refer to S.D.S. for standard bearing clearance and available spare parts.

items.label: Fillet radius — CG10DE
items.condition: Crank pin and crank journal fillet

items.label: Fillet radius — CG13DE
items.condition: Crank pin and crank journal fillet

parts.name: Thrust bearing

parts.name: Lower bearing

model_notes: Fillet radius differs by engine: CG10DE R2.3-2.7 mm; CG13DE R1.5 mm
